About: It is a form of the strategy board game checkers.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the World Checkers Draughts Federation.
ConsistsOf: It consists of 12 pieces per side.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Jonathan Schaeffer.
HasA: It has pieces that move and capture diagonally forward.
It has pieces that are crowned when they reach the opposite end of the board.
It has pieces that can thereafter move and capture both backward and forward.
It has pieces that are traditionally black, red, or white.
It has enemy pieces that are captured by jumping over them.
It has men and kings as two classes of pieces.
It has men that are single pieces.
It has kings that consist of two men of the same colour, stacked one on top of the other.
It has a bottom piece that is referred to as crowned.
It has pieces with a crown molded, engraved or painted on one side.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by pieces moving and capturing diagonally forward.
It is characterized by pieces being crowned upon reaching the opposite end of the board.
It is characterized by crowned pieces that can move and capture backward and forward.
It is characterized by pieces being captured by jumping over them.
It is characterized by mandatory jumping.
It is characterized by multiple jumps being possible.
It is characterized by a king gaining the ability to move both forward and backward upon being crowned.
It is characterized by winning by capturing all opponent's pieces.
It is characterized by winning by leaving the opponent with no legal move.
It is characterized by a draw if neither side can force a win or by agreement.
HeldIn: It was held in the United States.
It was held in the British Isles.
It was held in South Africa.
IsA: It is a strategy board game.

It is a game played by two opponents.
It is a game where enemy pieces are captured by jumping over them.
It is a game that was weakly solved in 2007.
It is a game where both players can guarantee a draw with perfect play.
KnownAs: It is known as straight checkers.
KnownFor: It is known for its 8x8 variant being weakly solved in 2007.
It is known for the shortest possible game having twenty moves.
It is known for the three-move restriction variation.
It is known for the two-move restriction variation.
It is known for the Go As You Please (GAYP) style.
It is known for the huffing rule variation.
It is known for its standardized notation for recording games.
It is known for the men's World Championship dating to the 1840s.
It is known for the first English draughts computer program written by Christopher Strachey.

About: それは戦略ボードゲームであるチェッカーの一種です。
AssociatedWith: それはワールドチェッカーズドラフツ連盟と関連があります。
ConsistsOf: それは1サイドあたり12個の駒で構成されています。
DevelopedBy: それはジョナサン・シェーファーによって開発されました。
DevelopedIn: それはカナダで開発されました。
HasA: それは斜め前方に移動して駒を取る駒を持っています。
それはボードの反対側に到達するとクラウンされる駒を持っています。
それはクラウンされた後、後方および前方に移動して駒を取ることができる駒を持っています。
それは伝統的に黒、赤、または白の駒を持っています。
それは敵の駒を飛び越えて取る駒を持っています。
それは駒にはメンとキングの2種類があります。
それはメンは単一の駒です。
それはキングは同じ色の2つのメンを積み重ねたものです。
それは一番下の駒はクラウンされたと呼ばれます。
それは片側にクラウンが成形、刻印、または塗装された駒を持っています。
HasFeatureOf: それは斜め前方に移動して駒を取る駒によって特徴付けられます。
それはボードの反対側に到達すると駒がクラウンされることによって特徴付けられます。
それはクラウンされた駒が後方および前方に移動して駒を取ることができることによって特徴付けられます。
それは駒が飛び越えて取られることによって特徴付けられます。
それはジャンプが義務であることによって特徴付けられます。
それは複数のジャンプが可能であることによって特徴付けられます。
それはキングがクラウンされると前後移動能力を得ることによって特徴付けられます。
それは相手の駒をすべて取ることで勝利することによって特徴付けられます。
それは相手に合法的な動きがない状態にすることによって勝利することによって特徴付けられます。
それは引き分けが強制できない場合や合意による引き分けによって特徴付けられます。
